docs.title: TCG Pond
docs.text: TCG Pond is a dark-themed Pokémon Trading Card Game storefront built with React 19, TypeScript, Vite, and Tailwind CSS. It lets collectors browse hundreds of single cards and sealed products, add items to a persistent cart, authenticate with a simulated account system, and complete a multi-step checkout — all rendered client-side with no backend required out of the box.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Product catalogue — singles and sealed products with rarity, set, condition, and price metadata
  • Advanced filtering — filter by TCG, category, set, rarity, price range, and new arrivals; full-text search
  • Persistent cart — Zustand + localStorage keeps the cart between page reloads
  • Authentication — simulated login/register modal; swap for a real API when ready
  • Multi-step checkout — shipping address → payment details → order confirmation
  • Order history — persisted per user session; viewable on the Account page
  • Multi-TCG ready — architecture supports Pokémon, One Piece, Dragon Ball, Yu-Gi-Oh!, and MTG; enable with a single flag
  • Responsive — mobile-first design with a hamburger nav and touch-friendly controls

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Requirement Version Node.js 18 + npm 9 +

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# 1. Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/your-org/tcg-pond.git
cd tcg-pond

# 2. Install dependencies
npm install

# 3. Start the development server
npm run dev

# 4. Open in browser
# Navigate to http://localhost:5173

# 5. Build for production
npm run build

The dev server starts at http://localhost:5173 by default. The production build outputs to dist/.

docs.title: Project Layout
docs.text:
tcg-pond/
├── src/
│   ├── App.tsx                 ← Root component; router + layout shell
│   ├── main.tsx                ← React entry point
│   ├── types/index.ts          ← All shared TypeScript types
│   ├── data/
│   │   ├── products.ts         ← Product catalogue (raw CSV → Product[])
│   │   └── tcgConfig.ts        ← TCG tab definitions and feature flags
│   ├── store/
│   │   ├── cartStore.ts        ← Zustand cart store (persisted)
│   │   └── authStore.ts        ← Zustand auth/order store (persisted)
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── layout/             ← Header, Footer
│   │   ├── shop/               ← TCGTabs, ProductCard, ShopFilters
│   │   ├── cart/               ← CartDrawer
│   │   └── auth/               ← AuthModal
│   └── pages/
│       ├── HomePage.tsx
│       ├── ShopPage.tsx
│       ├── ProductDetailPage.tsx
│       ├── CartPage.tsx
│       ├── CheckoutPage.tsx
│       └── AccountPage.tsx
├── public/                     ← Static assets (favicon, icons)
├── tailwind.config.js
├── vite.config.ts
└── index.html

docs.title: Further Reading
docs.text:
• Architecture — component hierarchy, data flow, state management
  • Configuration — TCG flags, Tailwind tokens, environment variables
  • API Reference — stores, data helpers, and TypeScript types

docs.title: System Overview
docs.text: TCG Pond is a single-page application (SPA) with no server-side runtime. All data lives in-memory (the product catalogue is bundled JavaScript) and user state is persisted to localStorage via Zustand middleware. A real backend can be grafted in by replacing the simulated login/register functions in authStore.ts and introducing an API layer for products.

docs.title: Component Hierarchy
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[main.tsx] --> B[App.tsx]
    B --> C[BrowserRouter]
    C --> D[Header]
    C --> E[main — Routes]
    C --> F[Footer]
    C --> G[CartDrawer — global overlay]
    C --> H[AuthModal — global overlay]

    E --> P1[HomePage]
    E --> P2[ShopPage]
    E --> P3[ProductDetailPage]
    E --> P4[CartPage]
    E --> P5[CheckoutPage]
    E --> P6[AccountPage]

    P2 --> TC[TCGTabs]
    P2 --> SF[ShopFilters]
    P2 --> PC[ProductCard ×N]

    P1 --> PC2[ProductCard ×N]
    P3 --> PC3[ProductCard — related]

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    subgraph Data Layer
        PD[products.ts\nstatic catalogue]
        TC[tcgConfig.ts\nTCG feature flags]
    end

    subgraph State Layer
        CS[cartStore\nZustand + persist]
        AS[authStore\nZustand + persist]
    end

    subgraph UI Layer
        SHP[ShopPage]
        PDP[ProductDetailPage]
        CHK[CheckoutPage]
        ACC[AccountPage]
        CDR[CartDrawer]
        HDR[Header]
    end

    PD -->|products[]| SHP
    PD -->|getProductById| PDP
    TC -->|enabledTCGs| SHP

    SHP -->|addItem| CS
    PDP -->|addItem| CS
    CS -->|items, totalPrice| CDR
    CS -->|items, totalPrice| CHK
    CS -->|totalItems| HDR

    AS -->|user, orders| CHK
    AS -->|user, orders| ACC
    CHK -->|addOrder| AS

docs.title: State Management
docs.text:
Both stores use Zustand with the persist middleware. State is serialised to localStorage under a fixed key.

Store Key Persisted Fields cartStore tcg-pond-cart items authStore tcg-pond-auth user, orders

Stores are consumed directly via hooks in any component — no provider wrappers required.

docs.title: Routing
docs.text:
React Router v7 handles all navigation. Routes are defined in App.tsx:

Path Component Notes / HomePage Hero, new arrivals, categories, premium cards /shop ShopPage Full catalogue with filters, search, pagination /product/:id ProductDetailPage Single product view with related cards /cart CartPage Full cart page (mirrors CartDrawer) /checkout CheckoutPage Shipping → payment → confirmation /account AccountPage Profile view /account/orders AccountPage Order history (same component, path-based tab)

Filter state is encoded in the URL query string (e.g. ?tcg=pokemon&category=single&sort=price-asc) so links are shareable and the browser back button works correctly.

docs.title: Product Data Pipeline
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    RAW["RawRow[] (tuple array in products.ts)"]
    PARSE["parse() — derives id, category, price, isNew"]
    PRODUCTS["Product[] (exported)"]
    HELPERS["getProductById()\ngetUniqueSets()\ngetUniqueRarities()"]

    RAW --> PARSE --> PRODUCTS --> HELPERS

The catalogue is a compact tuple array (RawRow[]) that is parsed once at module load time into the typed Product[]. This avoids JSON overhead and keeps the source easy to edit as a CSV-like structure.

isNew is computed at parse time: a product is "new" if its dateAdded is within 30 days of the reference date (2026-04-06).

category is derived from whether cardNumber is empty: an empty cardNumber means the item is a sealed product.

price resolves to priceOverride when non-zero, otherwise marketPrice.

docs.title: Styling Architecture
docs.text:
• Tailwind CSS v3 with a custom design-token extension in tailwind.config.js
  • The pond-* colour palette provides the dark background system
  • Per-TCG colour pairs (pokemon.primary, pokemon.accent, etc.) allow dynamic tab/badge theming
  • Custom animations fade-in and slide-in are used for the AuthModal and CartDrawer respectively
  • No CSS-in-JS; all styling is utility-class based

docs.title: Key Technical Decisions
docs.text: Decision Rationale Static product data No backend required; easy CSV-style edits; instant load Zustand over Redux Minimal boilerplate; direct hook consumption; built-in persist middleware URL query string for filters Shareable URLs; free undo/redo via browser history Simulated auth Allows full UI flow without a backend; replace login/register bodies only Per-TCG feature flags Enables/disables entire game tabs without deleting data Shipping threshold logic Free shipping ≥ $50; calculated purely client-side at checkout

docs.title: TCG Feature Flags
docs.text:
TCG tabs are controlled by the TCG_CONFIG array in src/data/tcgConfig.ts. Set enabled: true to make a game appear in the shop navigation.

// src/data/tcgConfig.ts
export const TCG_CONFIG: TCGConfig[] = [
  { key: 'pokemon',    label: 'Pokémon',             primary: '#CC0000', accent: '#FFCB05', enabled: true  },
  { key: 'onePiece',   label: 'One Piece',            primary: '#E31E24', accent: '#1a56db', enabled: false },
  { key: 'dragonball', label: 'Dragon Ball',          primary: '#FF7F00', accent: '#FFD700', enabled: false },
  { key: 'yugioh',     label: 'Yu-Gi-Oh!',            primary: '#6B21A8', accent: '#DBA309', enabled: false },
  { key: 'mtg',        label: 'Magic: The Gathering', primary: '#1D4ED8', accent: '#D97706', enabled: false },
];
Field Type Description key TCGKey Internal identifier; used as URL param and product field label string Human-readable tab label primary string (hex) Tab border / badge background colour accent string (hex) Secondary highlight colour enabled boolean true = visible in shop; false = hidden

When only one TCG is enabled, the TCGTabs component renders nothing (no single-item tab strip).

docs.title: "New Arrivals" Threshold
docs.text:
A product is flagged isNew = true when its dateAdded falls within 30 days of the reference date hard-coded in src/data/products.ts:

const REFERENCE_DATE = '2026-04-06';
const NEW_WINDOW_DAYS = 30;

To update the threshold, edit these two constants.

docs.title: Shipping Rules
docs.text:
Free shipping is applied when the cart subtotal reaches a threshold defined in CheckoutPage.tsx:

const FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD = 50;   // USD
const SHIPPING_COST = 4.99;           // USD

const shipping = totalPrice() >= FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD ? 0 : SHIPPING_COST;

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text:
The shop page shows 24 products per page. Change PAGE_SIZE in src/pages/ShopPage.tsx:

const PAGE_SIZE = 24;

docs.title: Tailwind Design Tokens
docs.text: All custom tokens live in tailwind.config.js.

docs.title: pond colour palette (dark UI)
docs.text: Token Hex Usage pond-bg #080c18 Page background pond-surface #0f1623 Header, footer, modal backgrounds pond-card #151d2e Card backgrounds, inputs pond-border #1e2a3a Borders, dividers pond-muted #4a5568 Disabled text, placeholders pond-text #e2e8f0 Primary text pond-subtle #94a3b8 Secondary / label text

docs.title: TCG colour pairs
docs.text: Token Primary Accent pokemon #CC0000 #FFCB05 onepiece #E31E24 #1a56db dragonball #FF7F00 #FFD700 yugioh #6B21A8 #DBA309 mtg #1D4ED8 #D97706

docs.title: Custom shadows
docs.text: Token Value Usage shadow-card 0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.5) Product card hover shadow shadow-glow 0 0 20px rgba(204,0,0,0.3) Hero CTA button glow

docs.title: Custom animations
docs.text: Token Definition Used by animate-fade-in opacity 0→1, 200ms ease-out AuthModal animate-slide-in translateX(100%→0), 250ms ease-out CartDrawer

docs.title: localStorage Keys
Environment Variables
docs.text:
TCG Pond has no required environment variables in its default (static) configuration. The Stripe SDK packages are listed as dependencies but are not wired up — they are placeholders for a future real payment integration.

When you add a backend, create a .env file at the project root:

# Example — not required today
VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://api.example.com
VITE_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...

All VITE_ prefixed variables are exposed to the browser bundle by Vite.

docs.title: Build & Dev Scripts
docs.text: Script Command Description dev vite Start dev server with HMR on localhost:5173 build tsc -b && vite build Type-check then produce production bundle in dist/ preview vite preview Serve the dist/ build locally lint eslint . Run ESLint across all source files

docs.title: TypeScript Configuration
docs.text:
Three tsconfig files are used:

File Purpose tsconfig.json Root; references the two below tsconfig.app.json Browser source (src/) — targets ES2020, strict mode tsconfig.node.json Vite config file — targets ESNext, Node types

docs.title: Documentation System
docs.text: This project's documentation is automatically generated by doc-assistant — a Claude-powered documentation tool that runs on every git push.

docs.title: What runs on every push
docs.text:
Task What it produces Technical docs docs/ — architecture, API reference, configuration User guides docs/guides/ — getting started, usage, deployment Changelog CHANGELOG.md — version history from git commits HTML site site/index.html — this navigable site

All tasks run in the background so your push completes immediately. Progress is logged to .doc-assistant.log at the project root.

docs.title: Running manually
docs.text:
# From the project root
python ~/Projects/doc-assistant/main.py docs      .
python ~/Projects/doc-assistant/main.py guide     .
python ~/Projects/doc-assistant/main.py changelog .

# Rebuild the HTML site
python ~/Projects/doc-assistant/build_docs.py .

docs.title: Adding screenshots
docs.text:
Place screenshots in docs/assets/screenshots/ and embed them in any page:

![Login screen](../assets/screenshots/login.png)

See docs/assets/screenshots/README.md for naming conventions and format guidelines.

docs.title: Customising the documentation
docs.text: File Purpose mkdocs.yml Site structure, theme, navigation ~/Projects/doc-assistant/prompts/docs.md System prompt for technical docs ~/Projects/doc-assistant/prompts/guides.md System prompt for user guides ~/Projects/doc-assistant/prompts/changelog.md System prompt for changelog

docs.title: Viewing the site locally
docs.text:
# Serve with live reload (from project root)
mkdocs serve

# Or just open the built output directly
start site/index.html

docs.title: Source
docs.text: doc-assistant lives at ~/Projects/doc-assistant/. Its own documentation is at ~/Projects/doc-assistant/site/index.html.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page documents every public type, store, data helper, and component prop interface in TCG Pond.

docs.title: TCGKey
docs.text:
type TCGKey = 'pokemon' | 'onePiece' | 'dragonball' | 'yugioh' | 'mtg';

Union of all supported trading card game identifiers. Used as the tcg field on Product and as a URL query parameter.

docs.title: ProductCategory
docs.text:
type ProductCategory = 'single' | 'sealed';
  • 'single' — an individual trading card
  • 'sealed' — a factory-sealed product (booster pack, box, etc.)

docs.title: CardCondition
docs.text:
type CardCondition =
  | 'Near Mint'
  | 'Lightly Played'
  | 'Moderately Played'
  | 'Heavily Played'
  | 'Damaged';

Standard TCG grading scale from best (Near Mint) to worst (Damaged).

docs.title: OrderStatus
docs.text:
type OrderStatus = 'pending' | 'processing' | 'shipped' | 'delivered';

Lifecycle stages of an order. Currently set to 'pending' at checkout creation.

docs.title: TCGConfig
docs.text:
interface TCGConfig {
  key: TCGKey;      // Internal identifier
  label: string;    // Display name (e.g. "Pokémon")
  primary: string;  // Primary brand colour (hex)
  accent: string;   // Accent brand colour (hex)
  enabled: boolean; // Whether to show this TCG in the shop
}

One entry per supported game in tcgConfig.ts.

docs.title: Product
docs.text:
interface Product {
  id: string;              // Derived: "<tcg>-<set slug>-<cardNumber or name slug>"
  name: string;            // Card or product name
  tcg: TCGKey;             // Which game this belongs to
  set: string;             // Set/expansion name
  cardNumber?: string;     // Collector number (omitted for sealed)
  rarity?: string;         // Rarity tier (omitted for sealed)
  variance?: string;       // Print variant (e.g. "Holofoil", "Normal")
  condition: CardCondition;
  price: number;           // USD — priceOverride if set, else marketPrice
  stock: number;           // Units available
  category: ProductCategory;
  isNew: boolean;          // true if dateAdded within 30 days of reference date
  dateAdded: string;       // ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD)
}

docs.title: CartItem
docs.text:
interface CartItem {
  product: Product;
  quantity: number;
}

docs.title: Address
docs.text:
interface Address {
  name: string;    // Recipient full name
  street: string;  // Street address
  city: string;
  zip: string;     // ZIP or postal code
  country: string;
}

docs.title: OrderItem
docs.text:
interface OrderItem {
  productId: string;
  name: string;
  price: number;    // Price at time of purchase
  quantity: number;
}

docs.title: Order
docs.text:
interface Order {
  id: string;          // Format: "ORD-<7 alphanumeric chars>"
  items: OrderItem[];
  total: number;       // Grand total including shipping
  status: OrderStatus;
  address: Address;
  createdAt: string;   // ISO 8601 datetime string
}

docs.title: User
docs.text:
interface User {
  id: string;    // btoa(email) — deterministic, not cryptographically secure
  email: string;
  name: string;
}

docs.title: Cart Store (src/store/cartStore.ts)
docs.text:
Zustand store. Access via the useCartStore hook.

import { useCartStore } from '../store/cartStore';

docs.title: State
docs.text: Field Type Description items CartItem[] Current cart contents (persisted) isOpen boolean Whether the CartDrawer overlay is visible

docs.title: addItem(product: Product): void
docs.text:
Adds a product to the cart. If it already exists, increments quantity up to product.stock. Also sets isOpen = true to show the drawer.

const { addItem } = useCartStore();
addItem(product);

docs.title: removeItem(productId: string): void
docs.text:
Removes the cart line for the given product ID entirely.

const { removeItem } = useCartStore();
removeItem('pokemon-ascended-heroes-038-217');

docs.title: updateQuantity(productId: string, quantity: number): void
docs.text:
Sets the quantity for a cart line. If quantity <= 0, the item is removed via removeItem.

const { updateQuantity } = useCartStore();
updateQuantity(productId, 3);

docs.title: clearCart(): void
docs.text: Empties the cart (used after a successful order).

docs.title: openCart(): void / closeCart(): void
docs.text: Show or hide the CartDrawer overlay.

docs.title: totalItems(): number
docs.text:
Returns the sum of all item quantities.

const count = useCartStore((s) => s.totalItems());

docs.title: totalPrice(): number
docs.text:
Returns the cart subtotal in USD.

const subtotal = useCartStore((s) => s.totalPrice());

docs.title: Auth Store (src/store/authStore.ts)
docs.text:
Zustand store. Access via the useAuthStore hook.

import { useAuthStore } from '../store/authStore';

docs.title: State
docs.text: Field Type Description user User \| null Currently signed-in user (null = guest) orders Order[] Order history for the current user isAuthOpen boolean Whether the AuthModal is visible authMode 'login' \| 'register' Which form the modal displays

docs.title: login(email: string, password: string): Promise<boolean>
docs.text:
Simulated login with a 600 ms delay. Accepts any non-empty email/password pair. Returns true on success and closes the modal.

Replace the function body with a real fetch call when a backend is available.

const { login } = useAuthStore();
const ok = await login('user@example.com', 'secret');

docs.title: register(name: string, email: string, password: string): Promise<boolean>
docs.text: Simulated registration. Returns true on success and closes the modal.

docs.title: logout(): void
docs.text: Clears user and orders from state and localStorage.

docs.title: addOrder(order: Order): void
docs.text:
Appends an order to the user's history. Called by CheckoutPage after payment.

const { addOrder } = useAuthStore();
addOrder(newOrder);

docs.title: openAuth(mode?: 'login' | 'register'): void
docs.text:
Opens the AuthModal. Defaults to 'login' mode.

openAuth('register'); // open the sign-up form

docs.title: closeAuth(): void
docs.text: Hides the AuthModal.

docs.title: products: Product[]
docs.text:
The full catalogue array, parsed from the internal RawRow[] tuple format at module load.

import { products } from '../data/products';

docs.title: getProductById(id: string): Product | undefined
docs.text:
Returns the product matching the given ID, or undefined if not found.

import { getProductById } from '../data/products';
const card = getProductById('pokemon-ascended-heroes-038-217');

docs.title: getUniqueSets(tcg?: string): string[]
docs.text:
Returns a sorted array of unique set names, optionally filtered to a specific TCG key.

import { getUniqueSets } from '../data/products';
const sets = getUniqueSets('pokemon');
// ['Aquapolis', 'Ascended Heroes', ...]

docs.title: getUniqueRarities(tcg?: string): string[]
docs.text:
Returns a sorted array of unique rarity strings, optionally filtered to a specific TCG key.

import { getUniqueRarities } from '../data/products';
const rarities = getUniqueRarities('pokemon');
// ['Common', 'Double Rare', 'Hyper Rare', ...]

docs.title: TCG_CONFIG: TCGConfig[]
docs.text: Master array of all TCG entries (enabled and disabled).

docs.title: enabledTCGs: TCGConfig[]
docs.text:
Filtered subset of TCG_CONFIG where enabled === true. Used by TCGTabs and ShopPage to determine which games to show.

import { enabledTCGs } from '../data/tcgConfig';

docs.title: getTCGConfig(key: string): TCGConfig | undefined
docs.text:
Looks up a TCGConfig by its key. Returns undefined if not found.

import { getTCGConfig } from '../data/tcgConfig';
const cfg = getTCGConfig('pokemon');
// { key: 'pokemon', label: 'Pokémon', primary: '#CC0000', ... }

docs.title: ProductCard
docs.text:
interface Props {
  product: Product;
}

Renders a product tile with rarity badge, name, set, price, stock count, and an "Add to Cart" button. Navigates to /product/:id on click.

docs.title: TCGTabs
docs.text:
interface Props {
  activeTCG?: TCGKey;
}

Renders a horizontally scrollable row of pill buttons, one per enabled TCG. Navigates to /shop?tcg=<key> on selection. Returns null when fewer than two TCGs are enabled.

docs.title: ShopFilters
docs.text: No external props. Reads and writes URL search params directly via useSearchParams. Calls getUniqueSets and getUniqueRarities with the current tcg param to populate dropdowns.

docs.title: CartDrawer
docs.text: No props. Reads state from useCartStore. Renders as a fixed right-side overlay with a backdrop. Returns null when isOpen is false.

docs.title: AuthModal
docs.text: No props. Reads state from useAuthStore. Renders as a centred modal overlay. Returns null when isAuthOpen is false.

docs.title: URL Query Parameters (ShopPage)
docs.text: Parameter Type Description tcg TCGKey Active game tab category 'single' \| 'sealed' Filter by product category rarity string Filter by rarity name set string Filter by set/expansion name sort 'price-asc' \| 'price-desc' \| 'name-asc' \| 'name-desc' \| 'new' Sort order q string Full-text search (name and set) new 'true' Show new arrivals only min number Minimum price filter max number Maximum price filter page number Current page (1-indexed, 24 per page)

docs.title: Screenshots
docs.text:
Place application screenshots in this folder and reference them in the documentation using:

![Description](../assets/screenshots/your-screenshot.png)

docs.title: Naming conventions
docs.text: Screen Suggested filename Main / home screen home.png Login / authentication login.png Dashboard dashboard.png Settings settings.png Feature-specific feature-<name>.png

docs.title: Recommended format
docs.text:
• Format: PNG (or WebP for smaller files)
  • Resolution: At least 1280 x 720 px
  • Annotations: Use a tool like Snagit, ShareX, or Greenshot to add callouts

docs.title: Deployment Guide
docs.text:
This guide covers how to build TCG Pond for production and deploy it to the most common static hosting platforms.

TCG Pond is a fully client-side single-page application — the production build is a folder of static HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files that can be served by any static host with no server-side runtime required.

docs.title: Building for Production
docs.text:
1. Run the build command from the project root:

    npm run build

    This command runs the TypeScript compiler first (tsc -b) and then Vite's production bundler. Output goes to dist/.

    Expected output (abridged):

    ✓ TypeScript compilation successful
vite v8.x.x building for production...
✓ 412 modules transformed.
dist/index.html            0.54 kB
dist/assets/index-xxxx.js  320.12 kB │ gzip: 98.45 kB
dist/assets/index-xxxx.css  28.71 kB │ gzip:  6.82 kB
✓ built in 4.21s

  2. Preview the build locally before deploying:

    npm run preview

    This starts a local server at http://localhost:4173 that serves the dist/ folder exactly as a CDN would. Verify the app works correctly before pushing to production.

    Tip

    Always run npm run preview after npm run build to catch any issues that only appear in the optimised bundle (tree-shaking, asset paths, etc.).

docs.title: SPA Routing Requirement
docs.text: Because TCG Pond uses React Router with client-side navigation, every URL must be redirected to index.html by the server. Each platform below has a dedicated configuration section for this.

docs.title: Netlify
docs.text:
Netlify is the simplest option — it detects the Vite framework automatically.

  1. Push your code to a GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository.

  2. Log in to netlify.com → Add new site → Import an existing project.

  3. Authorise Netlify to access your repository and select the repo.

  4. Set the build settings:

    Setting Value Build command npm run build Publish directory dist

  5. Click Deploy site.

    Expected outcome: Netlify runs the build and publishes the site to a URL such as https://tcg-pond-abc123.netlify.app.

  6. Configure SPA redirect. Create public/netlify.toml (or a _redirects file in public/):

    netlify.toml_redirects 
    [[redirects]]
  from = "/*"
  to = "/index.html"
  status = 200

    /*  /index.html  200

    Commit and push — Netlify redeploys automatically.

docs.title: Vercel
docs.text:
1. Push your code to a GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repository.

  2. Log in to vercel.com → Add New Project → import the repository.

  3. Vercel auto-detects Vite. Confirm the settings:

    Setting Value Framework preset Vite Build command npm run build Output directory dist

  4. Click Deploy.

    Vercel handles SPA routing automatically for Vite projects — no extra configuration needed.

    Expected outcome: The site is live at https://tcg-pond.vercel.app (or your custom domain).

docs.title: Cloudflare Pages
docs.text:
1. Log in to the Cloudflare dashboard → Workers & Pages → Create application → Pages → Connect to Git.

  2. Select your repository.

  3. Set build settings:

    Setting Value Framework preset Vite Build command npm run build Build output directory dist

  4. Click Save and Deploy.

  5. Configure SPA routing. Create public/_redirects:

    /*  /index.html  200

    Commit and push. Cloudflare Pages redeploys automatically.

docs.title: nginx
docs.text:
Use this approach when self-hosting on a VPS or bare-metal server.

  1. Build the app and copy the dist/ folder to your server (e.g. via rsync):

    npm run build
rsync -avz dist/ user@your-server:/var/www/tcg-pond/

  2. Create an nginx server block:

    server {
    listen 80;
    server_name tcg-pond.example.com;
    root /var/www/tcg-pond;
    index index.html;

    # SPA fallback — send all paths to index.html
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
    }

    # Cache static assets aggressively (they have content hashes in filenames)
    location /assets/ {
        expires 1y;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
    }
}

  3. Test and reload nginx:

    sudo nginx -t
sudo systemctl reload nginx

HTTPS

Use Certbot to obtain a free Let's Encrypt TLS certificate: 

sudo certbot --nginx -d tcg-pond.example.com

docs.title: Environment Variables (Optional)
docs.text:
TCG Pond has no required environment variables in its default static configuration. If you add a real backend, create a .env file at the project root before building:

VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://api.example.com
VITE_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_live_...

All VITE_-prefixed variables are inlined into the browser bundle at build time by Vite. Never store secrets (private keys, server credentials) in VITE_-prefixed variables.

Set the same variables as environment variables in your hosting platform's dashboard so that CI/CD builds pick them up without committing the .env file.

docs.title: Continuous Deployment
docs.text:
All three hosted platforms (Netlify, Vercel, Cloudflare Pages) support automatic deployments triggered by a git push to the default branch. No additional CI configuration is needed for the default setup.

For self-hosted deployments, consider a simple GitHub Actions workflow:

# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
name: Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - name: Deploy to server
        run: rsync -avz --delete dist/ user@your-server:/var/www/tcg-pond/
        # Configure SSH key in repository secrets

docs.title: Post-Deployment Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Home page loads and hero image renders correctly
  • [ ] Shop page shows product cards
  • [ ] Filtering and pagination work (check URL params update)
  • [ ] Adding a product to the cart opens the cart drawer
  • [ ] Checkout flow completes and shows the order confirmation screen
  • [ ] Direct URL navigation (e.g. /shop, /cart) works without a 404
  • [ ] Browser back/forward navigation works correctly

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: By the end of this guide you will have TCG Pond running locally in your browser, with the shop displaying products and the cart working end-to-end.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Requirement Version Notes Node.js 18 or later Check with node -v npm 9 or later Bundled with Node.js; check with npm -v Git Any recent version Only needed to clone the repo

No backend, database, or third-party API account is required to run the default configuration.

Node version managers

If you manage multiple Node versions, nvm (macOS / Linux) or nvm-windows make switching easy.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository.

    git clone https://github.com/your-org/tcg-pond.git
cd tcg-pond

    Expected outcome: You are now inside the tcg-pond/ directory.

  2. Install dependencies.

    npm install

    npm downloads React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, Zustand, React Router, and all dev tooling into node_modules/. This typically takes 15–30 seconds.

    Expected outcome: The command exits with no errors and you see a summary such as added 312 packages.

    Windows path lengths

    If you see ENAMETOOLONG errors on Windows, enable long path support: 

    git config --system core.longpaths true

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
TCG Pond works out of the box with no required environment variables. The only file you may want to inspect before first run is src/data/tcgConfig.ts, which controls which game tabs appear in the shop.

By default, only Pokémon is enabled:

// src/data/tcgConfig.ts
export const TCG_CONFIG: TCGConfig[] = [
  { key: 'pokemon', label: 'Pokémon', primary: '#CC0000', accent: '#FFCB05', enabled: true  },
  { key: 'onePiece', label: 'One Piece', ...,             enabled: false },
  { key: 'dragonball', label: 'Dragon Ball', ...,         enabled: false },
  { key: 'yugioh', label: 'Yu-Gi-Oh!', ...,               enabled: false },
  { key: 'mtg', label: 'Magic: The Gathering', ...,       enabled: false },
];

You don't need to change anything here to run the app — move on to the first run, then come back to explore the options.

docs.title: First Run
docs.text:
1. Start the development server.

    npm run dev

    Expected output:

      VITE v8.x.x  ready in 300 ms

  ➜  Local:   http://localhost:5173/
  ➜  Network: use --host to expose

  2. Open the app in your browser.

    Navigate to http://localhost:5173.

    You should see the dark-themed storefront with a hero section and navigation bar. Clicking Shop takes you to the product catalogue.

  3. Verify the shop loads.

    Go to Shop → you should see product cards for Pokémon singles and sealed products. The filter sidebar on the left lets you narrow by category, set, rarity, price, and new arrivals.

    📷 Screenshot: Shop page showing product grid with filter sidebar open

  4. Add an item to your cart.

    Click Add to Cart on any product card. A cart drawer slides in from the right confirming your item. The cart icon in the header shows a badge with the item count.

    Expected outcome: Cart drawer opens with one item; badge on the cart icon reads "1".

The development server supports Hot Module Replacement (HMR) — any change you save to a source file refreshes the relevant part of the app in the browser instantly without a full page reload.

docs.title: Stopping and Restarting
docs.text:
• Stop the server: press Ctrl+C in the terminal.
  • Restart at any time: run npm run dev again from the project root.
  • Your cart and auth state are persisted in localStorage, so they survive restarts.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: What you want to do Where to go Browse filtering, checkout, and account features Usage Guide Enable more TCG tabs or tune colours, prices, and pagination Configuration Deploy the site to production Deployment Guide Diagnose an error Troubleshooting Understand data flow and component structure Architecture

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: This page covers the most common problems encountered when installing, developing, building, or deploying TCG Pond.

docs.title: npm install fails with peer dependency errors
docs.text:
Symptom: The terminal shows ERESOLVE or peer dependency conflict errors.

Fix: Use the --legacy-peer-deps flag:

npm install --legacy-peer-deps

If the error persists, ensure you are on Node.js 18 or later:

node -v   # should print v18.x.x or higher

docs.title: npm install is very slow or hangs
docs.text:
Symptom: npm appears to stall, especially on the first install.

Fixes:

  • Check your internet connection.

  • Clear the npm cache and retry:

    npm cache clean --force
npm install

  • If you are behind a corporate proxy, configure it:

    npm config set proxy http://proxy.company.com:8080
npm config set https-proxy http://proxy.company.com:8080

docs.title: ENAMETOOLONG on Windows
docs.text:
Symptom: File paths inside node_modules exceed the Windows 260-character limit.

Fix: Enable long path support:

# Run as Administrator
git config --system core.longpaths true

Then delete node_modules and reinstall:

rmdir /s /q node_modules
npm install

docs.title: Port 5173 is already in use
docs.text:
Symptom:

Error: listen EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::5173

Fix: Either stop the process using the port, or start the server on a different port:

npm run dev -- --port 3000

docs.title: Blank page at http://localhost:5173
docs.text:
Symptom: The browser shows a white/blank page with no error in the UI.

Fixes:

  1. Open the browser developer console (F12 → Console tab) and look for JavaScript errors.
  2. Check the terminal where npm run dev is running for compilation errors.
  3. Hard-refresh the browser: Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows/Linux) or Cmd+Shift+R (macOS).

  4. Clear localStorage and reload:

    localStorage.clear();
location.reload();

docs.title: Changes not reflecting in the browser
docs.text:
Symptom: You edited a file but the browser still shows the old version.

Fixes:

  • Vite's HMR should update the page automatically. If it does not, try a manual refresh.
  • Confirm the dev server is still running in the terminal (no errors, no "disconnected" message).
  • Check that you saved the file (Vite watches disk writes, not unsaved buffer state).

docs.title: TypeScript errors in the terminal
docs.text:
Symptom: The dev server starts but prints red TypeScript errors.

Tip

Vite does not block the dev server on TypeScript errors — the app may still run with type errors present. However, npm run build will fail.

Fix: Read the error message carefully — it includes the file path and line number. Common causes:

  • Missing type import — add the import with the type keyword.
  • Wrong product tuple length — ensure all 10 fields are present when adding a row to products.ts.
  • Invalid TCG key — tcg values must be one of 'pokemon' | 'onePiece' | 'dragonball' | 'yugioh' | 'mtg'.

docs.title: npm run build fails with TypeScript errors
docs.text:
Symptom: The build exits with error TS... messages.

Fix: Fix all TypeScript errors before building. Run the type checker in watch mode to find issues quickly:

npx tsc --noEmit --watch

docs.title: Build output is unexpectedly large
docs.text:
Symptom: The dist/assets/index-xxxx.js file is larger than ~500 kB (gzipped).

Likely cause: A large data file (e.g. products.ts with thousands of rows) is included in the bundle.

Fix: Consider lazy-loading data or paginating at the data source rather than in the browser.

docs.title: Cart items disappear on reload
docs.text:
Symptom: Items you added to the cart are gone after a page refresh.

Likely causes and fixes:

  1. localStorage is disabled in the browser (e.g. in private/incognito mode with strict settings). Check: window.localStorage — if it throws, storage is blocked.
  2. The tcg-pond-cart key was manually removed. Re-add items to the cart.
  3. A browser extension is clearing storage. Disable extensions and retry.

docs.title: Auth state (login, orders) is lost on reload
docs.text:
Symptom: You are logged out unexpectedly, or your order history disappears.

Fix: Same causes as the cart issue above — check localStorage:

// Browser console
JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('tcg-pond-auth') || '{}')

If the value is null, localStorage is unavailable or was cleared.

docs.title: Products show "Out of Stock" for everything
docs.text:
Symptom: Every product card shows the out-of-stock state.

Likely cause: The stock field for all products in src/data/products.ts is 0.

Fix: Verify that product rows have a non-zero stock value:

['Pikachu ex', 'Scarlet & Violet Base', '057/217', 'Double Rare', 'Holofoil', 'Near Mint', 9.14, 0, 1, '2026-04-01'],
//                                                                                                   ^
//                                                                                              stock = 1

docs.title: No products appear in the shop
docs.text:
Symptom: The shop page is empty — no cards, no "no results" message, just blank.

Likely causes:

  1. Wrong TCG key on products. Every product's tcg field must match the key of an enabled TCG in tcgConfig.ts. Mismatched keys (e.g. 'Pokemon' vs 'pokemon') will cause products to be silently excluded.

  2. Stale filter state in the URL. Check the URL for unexpected query parameters (e.g. ?rarity=SomeRarityThatDoesntExist). Clear them by navigating to /shop.

  3. TCG disabled. Ensure enabled: true is set for the relevant entry in TCG_CONFIG.

docs.title: App loads but all routes except / return 404
docs.text:
Symptom: Navigating directly to /shop or /cart returns a 404 from the server.

Cause: The server is not configured to redirect all paths to index.html.

Fix: Add the SPA redirect rule for your platform:

Netlify (_redirects)Cloudflare Pages (_redirects)nginx 
/*  /index.html  200

/*  /index.html  200

location / {
    try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
}

Place _redirects in the public/ folder so Vite copies it to dist/ during the build.

docs.title: Assets return 404 after deployment
docs.text:
Symptom: The page loads but images or scripts return 404 errors.

Likely cause: The base path in vite.config.ts does not match the deployment subdirectory.

Fix: If the app is hosted at https://example.com/tcg-pond/, set the base:

// vite.config.ts
export default defineConfig({
  base: '/tcg-pond/',
  plugins: [react()],
})

Rebuild and redeploy after changing this value.

docs.title: Still Stuck?
docs.text:
1. Search the Vite issues tracker for your error message.
  2. Check the React Router docs for routing-specific problems.
  3. Open the browser DevTools Network tab to inspect failing requests.
  4. Check the Console tab for the exact JavaScript error and stack trace.

docs.title: Usage Guide
docs.text: This guide covers the everyday workflows in TCG Pond — browsing and filtering products, managing your cart, checking out, and viewing your order history. It also covers common catalogue maintenance tasks for developers.

docs.title: Browsing the Shop
docs.text: Navigate to Shop (header link or /shop). The page opens on the first enabled TCG tab.

docs.title: TCG tabs
docs.text: If more than one TCG is enabled, a tab row appears at the top of the shop. Click a tab to switch game catalogues. Each tab has its own set of products, filter state, and URL parameter (?tcg=pokemon).

docs.title: Search
docs.text: Type in the Search cards… box to filter by card name or set name. The search is case-insensitive and updates results immediately.

docs.title: Filters
docs.text:
Click the Filters button (funnel icon) on mobile or use the sidebar on desktop to open the filter panel. Available filters:

Filter What it does Category Singles only, sealed only, or both Set Restrict to one expansion set Rarity Common, Uncommon, Rare, Ultra Rare, etc. Price range Minimum and maximum price in USD New arrivals Only products added within the last 30 days

All filters are reflected in the URL query string, so you can bookmark or share a filtered view.

docs.title: Sorting
docs.text:
Use the Sort dropdown to order results by:

  • Price: low → high / high → low
  • Name: A → Z / Z → A
  • Newest first

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text: The shop shows 24 products per page. Use the numbered pagination controls at the bottom to move between pages. The current page is stored in the ?page= query parameter.

docs.title: Product Detail Page
docs.text:
Click any product card to open its detail page (/product/:id). The page shows:

  • Full card name, set, and card number
  • Rarity and variant (e.g. Holofoil)
  • Condition (Near Mint, Lightly Played, etc.)
  • Price and stock count
  • Add to Cart button (disabled when out of stock)
  • New Arrival badge if the card was added within the last 30 days

Use the browser Back button or the breadcrumb to return to the shop with your previous filters intact.

docs.title: Adding items
docs.text:
• Click Add to Cart on a product card or the product detail page.
  • The cart drawer opens automatically each time an item is added.
  • Adding the same product again increments its quantity (capped at available stock).

docs.title: Cart drawer
docs.text:
The drawer slides in from the right. From here you can:

  • Adjust quantity with the + / − buttons (quantity 0 removes the item)
  • Remove an item with the trash icon
  • See the running subtotal
  • Proceed to Checkout

docs.title: Cart page
docs.text: Navigate to /cart for the full-page cart view. It shows the same controls as the drawer plus a breakdown of items and totals.

docs.title: Persistence
docs.text:
The cart is saved to localStorage under the key tcg-pond-cart. It survives page reloads and browser restarts. To clear it manually, run this in the browser console:

localStorage.removeItem('tcg-pond-cart');

docs.title: Checkout
docs.text:
Login not required

You can check out as a guest — the site does not enforce authentication at checkout. However, your order will only be saved to the Account page if you are logged in.

docs.title: Step 1 — Shipping information
docs.text:
Fill in:

  • Full name
  • Street address
  • City
  • ZIP / postal code
  • Country

All fields are required. Click Continue to Payment to proceed.

docs.title: Step 2 — Payment details
docs.text:
Enter card details:

  • Card number (16 digits)
  • Expiry date (MM/YY)
  • CVC (3 digits)

No real payment processing

Stripe packages are installed as placeholders but no charges are made. Any 16-digit number, valid-looking expiry, and 3-digit CVC will be accepted. Do not enter real card data.

Click Place Order to complete the purchase.

docs.title: Step 3 — Order confirmation
docs.text:
A success screen displays your order ID (format ORD-XXXXXXX), the order total, and a link back to the shop. The cart is automatically cleared.

Free shipping is applied when the cart subtotal is $50.00 or more; otherwise a flat $4.99 shipping fee is added.

docs.title: Registering
docs.text:
1. Click the person icon in the header, or the Sign In / Register link.
  2. Switch to the Register tab.
  3. Enter your name, email address, and a password.
  4. Click Create Account.

Expected outcome: The modal closes and your name appears in the header. The simulated registration accepts any non-empty values.

docs.title: Logging in
docs.text:
1. Click the person icon in the header.
  2. Enter your email and password.
  3. Click Sign In.

The simulated login accepts any email/password combination, as long as neither field is empty. It reuses the same account (by email) across sessions.

docs.title: Order history
docs.text:
Navigate to /account while logged in. The page lists all orders placed in the current session, with:

  • Order ID
  • Date placed
  • Items and quantities
  • Shipping address
  • Order total
  • Status badge (pending, processing, shipped, delivered)

Tip

Order history is persisted in localStorage under tcg-pond-auth. It survives page reloads but is tied to the browser — it will not sync across devices.

docs.title: Logging out
docs.text: Click your name in the header → Sign Out. This clears the user session and order history from state (and from localStorage).

docs.title: Adding a single card
docs.text:
Open src/data/products.ts and append a row to the raw array inside the addTCG('pokemon') wrapper:

// Single card
['Charizard ex', 'Scarlet & Violet Base', '006/258', 'Double Rare', 'Holofoil', 'Near Mint', 89.99, 0, 2, '2026-04-06'],

Field order: name, set, cardNumber, rarity, variance, condition, marketPrice, priceOverride, stock, dateAdded

Field Notes cardNumber Leave '' for sealed products — this auto-sets category = 'sealed' priceOverride Set to a non-zero value to override marketPrice; 0 uses market price dateAdded YYYY-MM-DD — products within 30 days of the reference date get an isNew badge

docs.title: Adding a sealed product
docs.text:
// Sealed product — empty cardNumber
['Scarlet & Violet Booster Box', 'Scarlet & Violet Base', '', '', '', 'Near Mint', 149.99, 0, 5, '2026-04-06'],

docs.title: Enabling a new TCG tab
docs.text:
1. Open src/data/tcgConfig.ts.

  2. Set enabled: true for the desired game.

    { key: 'mtg', label: 'Magic: The Gathering', primary: '#1D4ED8', accent: '#D97706', enabled: true },

  3. Add products for that TCG to src/data/products.ts using the matching addTCG('mtg') wrapper.

  4. Save the files — the dev server will hot-reload and the new tab will appear in the shop.

See the Configuration reference for full details on TCG flags and design tokens.
